build_replicate_transcribe_translate_m: Build • Replicate • Transcribe • Translate • Mutate • CRISPR • Forensics
build_the_double_helix_from_4_letters: Build - the double helix from 4 letters
a_pairs_with_t_2_bonds_g_with_c_3_wats: A pairs with T (2 bonds), G with C (3). Watson + Crick + Franklin (1953) cracked the structure from X-ray crystallography. The shape itself encodes how DNA copies and reads.
helicase_splits_the_strands_dna_polyme: Helicase splits the strands; DNA polymerase synthesizes complements. Semi-conservative: each new helix has one old strand + one new. Meselson & Stahl proved it 1958.
transcribe_dna_mrna_in_the_nucleus: Transcribe - DNA → mRNA in the nucleus
rna_polymerase_reads_one_dna_strand_3_: RNA polymerase reads one DNA strand 3′→5′ and builds RNA 5′→3′. Same letters except T becomes U. mRNA carries the recipe out to the ribosomes - the central dogma’s first arrow.
translate_mrna_protein_at_the_ribosome: Translate - mRNA → protein at the ribosome
64_codons_20_amino_acids_the_genetic_c: 64 codons → 20 amino acids (the genetic code is redundant). AUG starts; UAA/UAG/UGA stop. tRNA reads codons, charges them with the right amino acid, peptide bond forms.
point_mutation_one_letter_swapped_inse: Point mutation: one letter swapped. Insertion/deletion: frameshift, the entire downstream protein is garbled. Sickle-cell is a single A→T - one letter, lifelong consequence.
bacterial_immune_system_repurposed_cas: Bacterial immune system, repurposed. Cas9 enzyme + guide RNA = molecular scissors. Doudna + Charpentier won the 2020 Nobel. First CRISPR therapy (sickle-cell) FDA-approved Dec 2023.
sequence_determines_structure_determin: Sequence determines structure determines function. Hemoglobin carries O₂, antibodies recognize, enzymes catalyze. AlphaFold (2020) predicted ~200M structures - a problem that took 50 years, cracked in 18 months.
codis_uses_20_short_tandem_repeat_str_: CODIS uses 20 short tandem repeat (STR) loci. Match probability is one-in-quadrillions when all loci agree. Innocence Project: 245+ wrongful convictions overturned by post-conviction DNA testing.
practice_transcription_translation_mut: Practice transcription, translation, mutation classification. AP Bio Big Idea 3.A.1: DNA, sometimes RNA, is the primary source of heritable information. NGSS HS-LS3-1.
retrieval_practice_gamified_speed_buil: Retrieval-practice gamified. Speed builds automaticity - once codon-table lookups are automatic, your working memory is free for higher-order thinking like predicting mutation impact.
dna_rna_protein_with_retroviruses_hiv_: DNA → RNA → protein - with retroviruses (HIV) running it backward via reverse transcriptase. Crick coined “dogma” in 1957; he later said he meant it as a hypothesis, not scripture.
pick_a_sequence_the_complementary_stra: Pick a sequence - the complementary strand fills in via base pairing (A-T, G-C). Real DNA is built like this constantly.
helicase_unwinds_the_helix_dna_polymer: Helicase unwinds the helix; DNA polymerase reads each template strand and lays down its complement. Semiconservative - each daughter has one old + one new strand.
rna_polymerase_reads_the_template_stra: RNA polymerase reads the template strand. Same complementary rules but T → U. Output: a single-stranded mRNA copy ready to leave the nucleus.
ribosome_reads_codons_3_bases_at_a_tim: Ribosome reads codons (3 bases at a time). Each codon → one amino acid. The genetic code is read in non-overlapping triplets, no commas.
point_mutations_silent_no_protein_chan: Point mutations: silent (no protein change), missense (one AA different), nonsense (premature stop). Frameshifts (insertion/deletion) are usually catastrophic.
guide_rna_points_cas9_to_a_20_base_tar: Guide RNA points Cas9 to a 20-base target. Cas9 cuts both strands. Cell repair pathways either knock out the gene or insert a template you provide.
primary_sequence_secondary_helix_sheet: Primary (sequence) → secondary (α-helix, β-sheet) → tertiary (3D fold) → quaternary (multi-subunit). One amino acid swap can break the fold.
str_profiling_13_20_short_tandem_repea: STR profiling: 13-20 short-tandem-repeat loci. Probability of a random match across all loci is roughly one in a billion. Used in CODIS, paternity, and crime labs.
a_new_dna_puzzle_every_session_transla: A new DNA puzzle every session. Translate, identify the mutation, find the ORF, or solve a forensic case. Streak counter tracks daily wins.
speed_translation_duel_decode_codons_f: Speed-translation duel. Decode codons fast against a timer. Tests whether the genetic code is in your head, not just your reference card.
codon_table_base_pairing_rules_key_ter: Codon table, base-pairing rules, key terms (ORF, intron, exon, promoter, repressor) - the cheat sheet you keep coming back to.
mrna_is_a_copy_of_the_coding_strand_wi: 💡 mRNA is a copy of the coding strand with U in place of T. (RNA Polymerase actually reads the complementary template strand 3'→5', which yields the same sequence as the coding strand.)
no_pam_sites_ngg_found_try_a_different: ⚠️ No PAM sites (NGG) found. Try a different DNA sequence from the Build tab.
double_strand_break_created_choose_rep: ✂️ Double-strand break created! Choose repair pathway:
crispr_clustered_regularly_interspaced: CRISPR (Clustered Regularly Interspaced Short Palindromic Repeats) is a gene editing technology adapted from bacteria's immune defense.
a_20nt_rna_that_directs_cas9_to_the_ta: - A ~20nt RNA that directs Cas9 to the target
protospacer_adjacent_motif_ngg_require: - Protospacer Adjacent Motif (NGG). Required for binding.
gene_therapy_sickle_cell_casgevy_cance: Gene therapy (sickle cell → CASGEVY™), cancer immunotherapy, crop engineering, disease models.
ala_val_leu_ile_pro_phe_trp_met_gly: Ala, Val, Leu, Ile, Pro, Phe, Trp, Met, Gly
ser_thr_cys_tyr_asn_gln: Ser, Thr, Cys, Tyr, Asn, Gln
predict_the_effect_type_from_5_options: Predict the effect type from 5 options.
10_mutation_vignettes_each_shows_a_sho: 10 mutation vignettes. Each shows a short before/after DNA sequence with the change highlighted. Pick the effect type - silent, missense, nonsense, frameshift, or in-frame indel. Coaching after each pick names what makes this effect more likely than the others.
polar_hydroxyl_side_chain_often_phosph: Polar, hydroxyl side chain. Often phosphorylated.
forms_disulfide_bonds_s_s_critical_for: Forms disulfide bonds (S-S). Critical for protein folding.
basic_side_chain_active_site_of_many_e: Basic side chain. Active site of many enzymes (pKa near 7).
basic_long_side_chain_often_acetylated: Basic, long side chain. Often acetylated/methylated.
basic_guanidinium_group_6_codons_most_: Basic, guanidinium group. 6 codons — most degenerate.
smallest_no_side_chain_high_flexibilit: Smallest. No side chain — high flexibility.
helicase_unwinds_the_double_helix_at_o: Helicase unwinds the double helix at origins of replication. Single-strand binding proteins prevent re-annealing. Topoisomerase relieves the supercoiling tension ahead of the fork.
primase_lays_down_short_10_nt_rna_prim: Primase lays down short (~10 nt) RNA primers — DNA polymerase needs a 3'-OH to extend from, so it cannot start a new strand alone.
synthesized_continuously_5_3_in_the_sa: Synthesized continuously 5'→3' in the same direction as the replication fork moves. One primer per origin.
synthesized_in_short_100_1000_nt_fragm: Synthesized in short 100–1000 nt fragments (Okazaki fragments) because polymerase can only extend 5'→3'. Each fragment needs its own primer.
rna_primers_are_excised_and_replaced_w: RNA primers are excised and replaced with DNA nucleotides.
joins_okazaki_fragments_by_forming_the: Joins Okazaki fragments by forming the final phosphodiester bond.
polymerase_checks_each_base_mismatches: Polymerase checks each base; mismatches removed and replaced. Lowers error rate from ~10⁻⁵ to ~10⁻⁷.
catches_the_few_mismatches_that_escape: Catches the few mismatches that escape proofreading. Final error rate: ~10⁻¹⁰ per base pair.
rna_polymerase_binds_the_promoter_e_g_: RNA polymerase binds the promoter (e.g., TATA box ~25 bp upstream of transcription start). Helps melt the DNA double helix.
polymerase_reads_template_strand_3_5_s: Polymerase reads template strand 3'→5', synthesizing mRNA 5'→3'. Adds ~30 nucleotides per second in bacteria; ~10-50 in eukaryotes.
bacteria_rho_dependent_or_intrinsic_te: Bacteria: rho-dependent or intrinsic termination (hairpin loop). Eukaryotes: cleavage and polyadenylation signals.
7_methylguanosine_cap_added_to_5_end_h: 7-methylguanosine cap added to 5' end. Helps ribosome recognize mRNA + protects from degradation.
introns_removed_exons_joined_by_the_sp: Introns removed, exons joined by the spliceosome. Alternative splicing → multiple proteins from one gene.
100_250_adenine_nucleotides_added_to_3: ~100-250 adenine nucleotides added to 3' end. Stabilizes mRNA + signals export.
mature_mrna_exits_through_nuclear_pore: Mature mRNA exits through nuclear pore complexes to the cytoplasm.
small_ribosomal_subunit_binds_mrna_ini: Small ribosomal subunit binds mRNA. Initiator tRNA (carrying Met) binds AUG start codon. Large subunit joins.
each_cycle_aminoacyl_trna_enters_a_sit: Each cycle: aminoacyl-tRNA enters A site → peptide bond forms (catalyzed by 23S rRNA, a ribozyme) → ribosome translocates one codon → tRNA shifts A→P→E.
stop_codon_uaa_uag_uga_recognized_by_r: Stop codon (UAA/UAG/UGA) recognized by release factors. Peptide released, ribosome dissociates.
chaperones_hsp70_groel_help_proteins_f: Chaperones (Hsp70, GroEL) help proteins fold correctly. Post-translational modifications: phosphorylation, glycosylation, cleavage, disulfide bonds.
codon_changes_but_amino_acid_is_the_sa: Codon changes but amino acid is the same (genetic code redundancy). No effect.
codon_changes_different_amino_acid_eff: Codon changes → different amino acid. Effect ranges from none (conservative substitution) to severe (sickle cell: Glu→Val).
codon_changes_stop_codon_truncated_pro: Codon changes → STOP codon. Truncated protein, usually non-functional.
adds_nucleotide_s_if_not_a_multiple_of: Adds nucleotide(s). If not a multiple of 3, shifts reading frame → all downstream codons changed. Usually severe.
removes_nucleotide_s_same_frameshift_c: Removes nucleotide(s). Same frameshift consequence as insertion.
extra_copies_of_a_gene_or_region_can_l: Extra copies of a gene or region. Can lead to dosage effects (Down syndrome = extra chrom 21).
segment_of_chromosome_flipped_may_disr: Segment of chromosome flipped. May disrupt genes at breakpoints.
segment_moves_to_a_different_chromosom: Segment moves to a different chromosome. Famous: Philadelphia chromosome (CML; BCR-ABL fusion).
repeating_unit_e_g_cag_expands_across_: Repeating unit (e.g., CAG) expands across generations. Huntington's (CAG repeats in HTT), fragile X (CGG in FMR1), myotonic dystrophy.
pairs_1_22_numbered_roughly_by_size_wi: Pairs 1-22 (numbered roughly by size, with exceptions: chr 21 is smaller than chr 22).
xx_female_or_xy_male_y_is_much_smaller: XX (female) or XY (male). Y is much smaller, carries few genes (~70) vs X (~800).
23_pairs_diploid_in_somatic_cells_game: 23 pairs (diploid in somatic cells). Gametes are haploid (23 chromosomes, no pairs).
surprisingly_few_many_genes_produce_mu: Surprisingly few — many genes produce multiple proteins via alternative splicing. Much of the genome is regulatory or non-coding.
if_stretched_out_1_8_m_of_dna_per_cell: If stretched out, ~1.8 m of DNA per cell. Packed into a nucleus ~10 µm across via histones + supercoiling.
maternally_inherited_codes_for_37_gene: Maternally inherited. Codes for 37 genes (13 proteins, 22 tRNAs, 2 rRNAs). Multiple copies per mitochondrion.
amplifies_a_specific_dna_segment_by_2_: Amplifies a specific DNA segment by 2ⁿ (n cycles). Uses heat-stable Taq polymerase. Foundation of modern molecular biology.
reads_dna_sequence_using_chain_termina: Reads DNA sequence using chain-terminating dideoxynucleotides. Read length ~800-1000 bp. Now largely replaced by next-gen sequencing.
massively_parallel_sequences_millions_: Massively parallel — sequences millions of fragments simultaneously. Illumina dominates. ~100-300 bp reads, billions per run.
programmable_gene_editing_guide_rna_ta: Programmable gene editing. Guide RNA targets Cas9 nuclease to specific DNA. Cuts allow gene knockout or HDR-mediated insertion. Nobel 2020.
edits_a_single_base_without_double_str: Edits a single base without double-strand break. Lower off-target effects than Cas9.
bacterial_proteins_that_cut_dna_at_spe: Bacterial proteins that cut DNA at specific 4-8 bp sequences. Enabled recombinant DNA technology. Nobel 1978.
joins_dna_fragments_via_phosphodiester: Joins DNA fragments via phosphodiester bonds. T4 DNA ligase is the workhorse for cloning.
separates_dna_fragments_by_size_in_an_: Separates DNA fragments by size in an electric field. Smaller fragments migrate faster through gel matrix.
thousands_of_dna_probes_on_a_chip_meas: Thousands of DNA probes on a chip. Measure gene expression or detect SNPs in parallel.
sequences_mrna_from_individual_cells_r: Sequences mRNA from individual cells. Reveals cell-type heterogeneity that bulk RNA-seq misses.
near_atomic_structures_of_rna_dna_comp: Near-atomic structures of RNA/DNA complexes without crystallization. Particularly powerful for ribosomes, spliceosomes, replisomes.
read_lengths_up_to_100kb_vs_illumina_s: Read lengths up to 100kb+ (vs Illumina's ~300bp). Better for structural variants, repeats, full-length transcripts.
differential_reproduction_based_on_her: Differential reproduction based on heritable variation. Genes that improve survival/reproduction become more common over generations.
random_source_of_new_genetic_variation: Random source of new genetic variation. Most are neutral or harmful; rarely beneficial. Substrate for selection.
random_changes_in_allele_frequency_esp: Random changes in allele frequency, especially in small populations. Founder effect + bottlenecks are extreme cases.
movement_of_alleles_between_population: Movement of alleles between populations via migration. Reduces between-population differences; increases within-population diversity.
formation_of_new_species_usually_via_r: Formation of new species, usually via reproductive isolation. Allopatric (geographic separation) most common.
unrelated_organisms_evolve_similar_tra: Unrelated organisms evolve similar traits independently (wings in birds + bats + insects; eyes in vertebrates + cephalopods).
all_life_shares_a_common_ancestor_luca: All life shares a common ancestor (LUCA). Evidence: shared genetic code, ribosomes, ATP, glycolysis, basic biochemistry.
diagram_showing_evolutionary_relations: Diagram showing evolutionary relationships. Increasingly built from DNA sequence comparisons rather than morphology.
allele_frequencies_stay_constant_under: Allele frequencies stay constant under no-evolution assumptions: large pop, random mating, no selection/mutation/migration/drift. p² + 2pq + q² = 1.
interactive_references_pick_a_topic_be: Interactive references — pick a topic below to explore.
five_nitrogenous_bases_make_up_dna_rna: Five nitrogenous bases make up DNA + RNA: A, T, G, C (DNA); A, U, G, C (RNA). Purines (A, G) are 2-ringed; pyrimidines (T, U, C) are 1-ringed. Pairing follows specific H-bond patterns.
64_codons_4_encode_20_amino_acids_3_st: 64 codons (4³) encode 20 amino acids + 3 stop signals. Code is redundant (degenerate) — most amino acids have multiple codons, usually differing in the 3rd position ("wobble"). Universal across nearly all life.
third_codon_position_pairs_less_strict: Third codon position pairs less strictly — a single tRNA can recognize multiple codons. Reduces the number of tRNAs needed (cells have ~30-40, not 64).
semi_conservative_each_new_helix_has_o: Semi-conservative — each new helix has one parent + one newly-synthesized strand. Proven by Meselson-Stahl 1958. ~50 bp/sec in bacteria, ~50 bp/sec per fork in eukaryotes (but thousands of forks in parallel).
rna_polymerase_reads_dna_template_3_5_: RNA polymerase reads DNA template (3'→5') and synthesizes mRNA (5'→3'). In eukaryotes, the primary transcript is heavily processed before export.
ribosome_reads_mrna_in_3_base_codons_a: Ribosome reads mRNA in 3-base codons and builds protein. ~10-20 amino acids per second in bacteria; ~3-5 in eukaryotes. Multiple ribosomes can translate the same mRNA (polyribosome).
mutations_range_from_invisible_silent_: Mutations range from invisible (silent) to lethal. Their effect depends on what changes, where, and in which cell type (germline vs somatic).
a_sampling_of_well_characterized_genet: A sampling of well-characterized genetic disorders. Genetic counseling resources and current treatments evolve — verify with current literature for clinical decisions.
family_a_three_generations_blue_eyes: Family A — three generations, blue eyes
two_unaffected_parents_gen_i_have_one_: Two unaffected parents (gen I) have one affected child + one unaffected (gen II). The affected child + unaffected partner have one affected + one unaffected (gen III). Trait appears in roughly 25% of offspring of unaffected x unaffected matings.
trait_skips_generations_unaffected_par: Trait skips generations (unaffected parents → affected child).
parents_must_be_heterozygous_carriers_: Parents must be heterozygous carriers passing one recessive allele each.
affected_individuals_appear_in_both_se: Affected individuals appear in both sexes at roughly equal frequency.
affected_aa_homozygous_carriers_aa_are: Affected = aa homozygous; carriers (Aa) are phenotypically unaffected.
family_b_color_blindness_across_three_: Family B — color blindness across three generations
affected_grandfather_gen_i_has_unaffec: Affected grandfather (gen I) has unaffected daughter (gen II) and affected grandson via that daughter (gen III). No affected sons of unaffected fathers. Trait far more common in males.
trait_shows_strong_male_bias_hemizygou: Trait shows strong male bias (hemizygous males express; females need two copies).
unaffected_daughter_of_affected_grandf: Unaffected daughter of affected grandfather is an obligate carrier.
trait_skips_generation_through_carrier: Trait skips generation through carrier daughters, then reappears in grandsons.
no_father_to_son_transmission_sons_inh: No father-to-son transmission (sons inherit Y from father, not X).
every_affected_individual_has_at_least: Every affected individual has at least one affected parent. Trait appears in every generation. ~50% of children of affected parents are affected. Equal in males + females.
trait_appears_in_every_generation_no_s: Trait appears in every generation (no skipping).
every_affected_individual_has_at_least_2: Every affected individual has at least one affected parent.
roughly_50_of_children_of_an_affected_: Roughly 50% of children of an affected heterozygote are affected.
equal_frequency_in_males_females_autos: Equal frequency in males + females — autosomal, not sex-linked.
inheritance_pattern_reasoning_ranking_: Inheritance pattern + reasoning ranking — both must be committed before reveal. The reveal grades your ranking position-by-position, not just your final answer. Real inquiry requires you to know WHY, not just WHAT.
notice_the_ranking_grade_is_independen: Notice: the ranking grade is independent of the pattern grade — you can identify the WHAT correctly but rank the WHY out of order.
during_gamete_formation_the_two_allele: During gamete formation, the two alleles for each gene separate so each gamete carries only one allele.
alleles_for_different_genes_assort_ind: Alleles for different genes assort independently of one another during gamete formation (genes on different chromosomes).
when_two_different_alleles_are_present: When two different alleles are present, one (dominant) is expressed while the other (recessive) is masked.
methyl_group_ch_added_to_cytosines_esp: Methyl group (–CH₃) added to cytosines, especially at CpG islands. Generally silences gene expression.
acetylation_methylation_phosphorylatio: Acetylation, methylation, phosphorylation of histone tails. Changes chromatin packing.
atp_dependent_complexes_slide_or_eject: ATP-dependent complexes slide or eject nucleosomes to expose DNA.
mirna_lncrna_regulate_gene_expression_: miRNA, lncRNA regulate gene expression post-transcriptionally.
some_genes_expressed_only_from_materna: Some genes expressed only from maternal or paternal allele. Methylation marks.
some_epigenetic_marks_survive_gametoge: Some epigenetic marks survive gametogenesis and pass to offspring.
who_can_access_your_genome_gina_2008_u: Who can access your genome? GINA (2008, US) prohibits genetic discrimination in employment + health insurance, NOT life insurance.
23andme_ancestrydna_reveal_ancestry_he: 23andMe, AncestryDNA reveal ancestry + health risk. Concerns: incidental findings, accuracy, third-party data sharing.
in_2018_he_jiankui_edited_embryos_for_: In 2018, He Jiankui edited embryos for HIV resistance → twin births. Widely condemned. Most countries ban germline editing.
hypothetical_selection_or_editing_of_e: Hypothetical selection or editing of embryos for non-medical traits (height, IQ). Currently technically limited; ethically contested.
engineered_to_spread_through_wild_popu: Engineered to spread through wild populations (e.g., mosquito malaria-resistance). Potential ecological irreversibility.
should_we_test_for_untreatable_disease: Should we test for untreatable diseases (Huntington)? Some people choose not to know.
familial_dna_searches_solved_cold_case: Familial DNA searches solved cold cases (Golden State Killer, 2018) but raise privacy issues for relatives who never consented.
us_supreme_court_2013_ruled_that_natur: US Supreme Court (2013) ruled that naturally-occurring DNA cannot be patented, but synthetic cDNA can.
reference_genomes_historically_eurocen: Reference genomes historically Eurocentric. Improving reference diversity is ongoing (e.g., human pangenome project).
genome_size_does_not_correlate_with_or: Genome size does not correlate with organism complexity (C-value paradox). Humans have similar gene counts to roundworms — much of "extra" DNA in larger genomes is non-coding.
heritable_changes_in_gene_expression_t: Heritable changes in gene expression that don't involve changes to DNA sequence. Influenced by environment, diet, stress.
building_blocks_of_proteins_9_are_esse: Building blocks of proteins. 9 are "essential" (can't be synthesized — must come from diet): His, Ile, Leu, Lys, Met, Phe, Thr, Trp, Val.
essentially_no_beneficial_fixation_exp: Essentially no beneficial fixation expected. Population is in mutation-selection balance or drift-only.
drift_dominates_few_expected_fixations: Drift dominates: few expected fixations even though mutations occur. Common in small populations.
a_handful_of_beneficial_mutations_expe: A handful of beneficial mutations expected to fix. Standard for most natural populations.
many_fixations_expected_strong_selecti: Many fixations expected — strong selection, large population, or both. E.g., bacterial antibiotic resistance.
extreme_regime_viruses_cancer_cell_lin: Extreme regime: viruses, cancer cell lines, asexual lab populations. Many simultaneous adaptive sweeps.
set_per_base_mutation_rate_population_: Set per-base mutation rate, population size, generations, and selection coefficient. Predict how many beneficial mutations actually sweep to fixation. No score, no reveal.
log_n_vs_log_circles_typical_regimes: log₁₀(N) vs log₁₀(μ) — circles = typical regimes
your_hypothesis_which_slider_has_the_b: Your hypothesis (which slider has the biggest leverage on fixation rate? Why?)
e_g_selection_has_more_leverage_than_o: e.g., selection has more leverage than μ once N is large enough...
when_does_the_regime_move_from_drift_d: When does the regime move from drift-dominated to selection-dominated? What does N·s tell you?
why_are_antibiotic_resistance_and_vira: Why are antibiotic resistance and viral evolution so fast — which sliders are at their extreme?
a_neutral_mutation_s_0_has_fixation_pr: A neutral mutation (s = 0) has fixation probability 1/(2N). What does that mean for large populations?
could_the_same_population_size_be_drif: Could the same population size be "drift-dominated" for weak mutations and "selection-dominated" for strong ones simultaneously?
i_can_explain_why_this_n_g_s_combinati: I can explain why this μ × N × g × s combination yields this regime.
inquiry_widget_no_score_no_reveal_no_a: Inquiry widget — no score, no reveal, no answer dump. Uses the full Kimura (1962) diploid fixation formula (1−e^(−2s))/(1−e^(−4Ns)), which interpolates between Haldane's ~2s in the strong-selection limit (Ns≫1) and the neutral 1/(2N) in the drift limit (Ns≪1 or s=0).
more_than_humans_20_000_many_novel_gen: More than humans (~20,000). Many novel genes for sensing + cognition.
special_protein_dsup_wraps_dna_protect: Special protein (Dsup) wraps DNA + protects against X-ray damage. Studied for radiation therapy applications.
hypersensitive_contact_inhibition_high: Hypersensitive contact inhibition + high-molecular-weight hyaluronan. Cancer extremely rare.
elephants_have_20_copies_of_tumor_supp: Elephants have ~20 copies of tumor suppressor TP53 (humans have 1). Possible explanation for low cancer rates despite huge cell number.
axolotl_can_regrow_limbs_spinal_cord_h: Axolotl can regrow limbs, spinal cord, heart, even portions of brain. Studied for regenerative medicine.
stores_fat_not_water_myth_allows_survi: Stores fat, not water (myth). Allows survival without food for weeks. Water comes from drinking + metabolic byproduct.
population_crashed_12_000_years_ago_ge: Population crashed ~12,000 years ago. Genetic bottleneck → very low diversity. All cheetahs basically twins genetically.
modern_whales_descended_from_land_mamm: Modern whales descended from land mammals (~50 mya). Closest living relatives: hippos. Vestigial pelvis bones remain.
wide_variation_adder_s_tongue_fern_720: Wide variation. Adder's tongue fern: ~720 pairs (most ever). Rice: 12 pairs. Humans: 23 pairs.
all_female_asexual_for_80_million_year: All-female, asexual for ~80 million years. Acquire genes from other species (HGT) instead of mixing via sex.
lobe_finned_fish_thought_extinct_since: Lobe-finned fish thought extinct since dinosaur age. Living specimens found 1938. Lineage ~400 million years old.
blue_copper_based_hemocyanin_detects_b: Blue (copper-based hemocyanin). Detects bacterial endotoxins — used to test vaccines + medical devices.
diverged_from_brown_bears_150_000_500_: Diverged from brown bears ~150,000-500,000 years ago. Some grizzly-polar hybrids ("pizzly" or "grolar") confirmed in wild.
dog_breed_genetics_wild_dna_facts: 🐕 Dog breed genetics + wild DNA facts
hormones_travel_through_the_bloodstrea: Hormones travel through the bloodstream to act on distant target tissues. They orchestrate metabolism, growth, reproduction, stress response, and homeostasis.
approximate_elemental_composition_of_t: Approximate elemental composition of the human body by mass. Just 11 elements account for >99%. CHNOPS (C, H, N, O, P, S) make up almost all organic molecules.
recent_extinctions_mostly_human_caused: Recent extinctions (mostly human-caused) + notable prehistoric extinctions. Earth has had 5 mass extinctions — many argue we're in a 6th now.
6co_6h_o_light_c_h_o_6o_powered_by_chl: 6CO₂ + 6H₂O + light → C₆H₁₂O₆ + 6O₂. Powered by chlorophyll. Source of most atmospheric oxygen.
c3_most_plants_c4_corn_sugarcane_effic: C3 (most plants), C4 (corn, sugarcane — efficient at high T), CAM (cacti, succulents — open stomata at night to save water).
tiny_pores_on_leaves_let_co_in_o_water: Tiny pores on leaves. Let CO₂ in + O₂/water out. Open/close in response to light, water, CO₂.
carries_water_minerals_up_from_roots_d: Carries water + minerals UP from roots. Dead cells. Drives transpiration pull.
carries_sugars_from_leaves_to_rest_of_: Carries sugars from leaves to rest of plant. Living cells. Bidirectional.
plant_hormone_phototropism_growing_tow: Plant hormone. Phototropism (growing toward light). Concentration on dark side → cells elongate → bends toward light.
gas_hormone_ripens_fruit_why_ripe_bana: Gas hormone. Ripens fruit. Why ripe bananas ripen others. Stress signaling.
promotes_stem_elongation_seed_germinat: Promotes stem elongation, seed germination, flowering.
promotes_cell_division_delays_leaf_sen: Promotes cell division. Delays leaf senescence.
stress_hormone_closes_stomata_when_wat: Stress hormone. Closes stomata when water-stressed. Promotes seed dormancy.
angiosperm_unique_one_sperm_fertilizes: Angiosperm unique: one sperm fertilizes egg (embryo), one fertilizes polar nuclei (endosperm — food for seed).
flower_shape_color_scent_match_pollina: Flower shape + color + scent match pollinator preferences. Some highly specialized (orchid + specific moth).
evolved_in_nutrient_poor_soils_venus_f: Evolved in nutrient-poor soils. Venus flytrap (snap), pitcher plants (pitfall), sundew (sticky).
symbiosis_with_fungi_fungi_extend_root: Symbiosis with fungi. Fungi extend root reach; plants supply sugars. ~85% of plant species.
rhizobium_bacteria_in_root_nodules_con: Rhizobium bacteria in root nodules convert N₂ → NH₃. Why beans + peas are protein-rich + soil-improving.
domestication_selected_larger_seeds_wh: Domestication selected larger seeds (wheat), non-shattering (rice), sweeter fruit (apples). Recent: hybrid corn, GM, marker-assisted selection.
common_in_plants_rare_in_animals_wheat: Common in plants (rare in animals). Wheat is hexaploid (6 sets), strawberry is octoploid. Often increases vigor + crop yield.
stiffening_compound_in_cell_walls_of_w: Stiffening compound in cell walls of wood. Why wood is stiff + slow to decompose.
individuals_of_one_species_in_an_area: Individuals of one species in an area.
all_populations_interacting_in_an_area: All populations interacting in an area.
all_ecosystems_on_earth_the_zone_of_li: All ecosystems on Earth. The zone of life.
role_of_a_species_in_its_ecosystem_two: Role of a species in its ecosystem. Two species can't occupy exactly the same niche (competitive exclusion).
physical_place_where_an_organism_lives: Physical place where an organism lives.
network_of_who_eats_whom_trophic_level: Network of who eats whom. Trophic levels: producers → primary consumers → secondary → etc.
make_own_food_plants_algae_some_bacter: Make own food. Plants, algae, some bacteria. Foundation of food chains.
get_food_by_eating_others_herbivores_c: Get food by eating others. Herbivores, carnivores, omnivores.
break_down_dead_matter_fungi_bacteria_: Break down dead matter. Fungi, bacteria, detritivores. Recycle nutrients.
close_interaction_between_species_mutu: Close interaction between species. Mutualism (+/+), commensalism (+/0), parasitism (+/−).
disproportionate_effect_on_ecosystem_d: Disproportionate effect on ecosystem despite low abundance. Sea otters control urchins, which control kelp.
maximum_population_size_environment_ca: Maximum population size environment can sustain. Resources, predation, disease limit K.
population_grows_at_constant_rate_dn_d: Population grows at constant rate. dN/dt = rN. Cannot continue forever.
growth_slows_as_n_approaches_k_dn_dt_r: Growth slows as N approaches K. dN/dt = rN(K-N)/K. S-shaped curve.
many_offspring_little_parental_care_sh: Many offspring, little parental care, short-lived. Insects, weeds. Boom-and-bust.
few_offspring_lots_of_care_long_lived_: Few offspring, lots of care, long-lived. Whales, humans, oaks.
predictable_changes_in_community_over_: Predictable changes in community over time. Primary (bare rock) vs secondary (after disturbance).
variety_of_life_species_richness_count: Variety of life. Species richness (count) + evenness (distribution). High biodiversity → ecosystem resilience.
background_rate_1_species_per_million_: Background rate ~1 species per million per year. Current rate 100-1000× background due to humans.
major_life_zone_defined_by_climate_veg: Major life zone defined by climate + vegetation. Tropical rainforest, desert, tundra, savanna, etc.
n_nh_fixation_no_no_nitrification_orga: N₂ → NH₃ (fixation) → NO₂⁻ → NO₃⁻ (nitrification) → organic N → back to N₂ (denitrification).
co_photosynthesis_organic_c_respiratio: CO₂ ↔ photosynthesis ↔ organic C ↔ respiration. Fossil fuels move long-buried C back to atmosphere.
evaporation_condensation_precipitation: Evaporation → condensation → precipitation → runoff/groundwater → back to ocean.
movement_of_elements_c_n_p_s_through_b: Movement of elements (C, N, P, S) through biotic + abiotic compartments.
each_chosen_for_properties_that_make_b: Each chosen for properties that make biology tractable: small size, fast life cycle, ease of culture, genetic tools.
heat_separates_double_stranded_dna_int: Heat separates double-stranded DNA into single strands. Hydrogen bonds break.
primers_short_dna_sequences_bind_to_co: Primers (short DNA sequences) bind to complementary regions on each template strand.
dna_polymerase_taq_from_thermus_aquati: DNA polymerase (Taq, from Thermus aquaticus) extends primers, synthesizing new DNA.
each_cycle_doubles_target_dna_30_cycle: Each cycle doubles target DNA. 30 cycles → ~10⁹-fold amplification.
kary_mullis_invented_pcr_1983_won_nobe: Kary Mullis invented PCR (1983); won Nobel Prize 1993.
from_thermophilic_bacterium_in_hot_spr: From thermophilic bacterium in hot springs. Survives 95°C denaturation step.
fluorescent_dyes_report_on_amplificati: Fluorescent dyes report on amplification in real time. Used for COVID tests, gene expression.
reverse_transcription_pcr_converts_rna: Reverse Transcription PCR. Converts RNA to cDNA first. Used for RNA viruses (COVID, HIV, flu).
partitions_sample_into_thousands_of_dr: Partitions sample into thousands of droplets. Counts presence/absence — highly accurate quantification.
diagnostics_forensics_gene_cloning_anc: Diagnostics, forensics, gene cloning, ancient DNA (Jurassic Park-style; real life, mostly < 1 million years).
first_crispr_therapy_fda_approved_dec_: First CRISPR therapy FDA-approved (Dec 2023). Edits patient's stem cells ex vivo.
crispr_engineers_t_cells_to_attack_can: CRISPR engineers T cells to attack cancer. Several Phase I/II trials.
crispr_knockout_libraries_reveal_which: CRISPR knockout libraries reveal which genes are essential for disease.
faster_than_traditional_knockout_mice_: Faster than traditional knockout mice. Used widely in research.
disease_resistant_rice_mushrooms_that_: Disease-resistant rice, mushrooms that don't brown, gluten-free wheat (still mostly research).
spread_sterility_or_malaria_resistance: Spread sterility or malaria-resistance through wild populations. Field trials being debated.
he_jiankui_2018_edited_human_embryos_m: He Jiankui (2018) edited human embryos → moral + scientific outcry. Currently banned in most countries.
human_body_has_10_human_cells_10_10_mi: Human body has ~10¹³ human cells + ~10¹³-10¹⁴ microbial cells. Roughly equal in number.
500_1_000_bacterial_species_in_healthy: 500-1,000+ bacterial species in healthy adult gut. Plus archaea, fungi, viruses.
microbiome_carries_100_more_genes_than: Microbiome carries ~100× more genes than human genome. Adds many metabolic capabilities.
gut_microbes_communicate_with_brain_vi: Gut microbes communicate with brain via vagus nerve, immune signals, metabolites. Linked to mood, behavior — though many specific claims overstated.
broad_spectrum_antibiotics_wipe_out_co: Broad-spectrum antibiotics wipe out commensals → may take months/years to recover. Some shifts persistent.
fecal_microbiota_transplant_fmt_cures_: Fecal microbiota transplant (FMT) cures recurrent C. difficile infection ~90% of time. Other applications still experimental.
varies_by_body_site_forehead_oily_domi: Varies by body site: forehead (oily, dominated by Cutibacterium acnes), gut (anaerobes), armpit (apocrine sweat + bacteria → body odor).
healthy_lactobacillus_dominant_low_ph_: Healthy: Lactobacillus dominant, low pH. Imbalance → bacterial vaginosis (BV).
700_species_affects_cavities_gum_disea: ~700 species. Affects cavities, gum disease. Linked to cardiovascular risk.
vaginal_birth_seeds_infant_with_matern: Vaginal birth seeds infant with maternal vaginal microbes; C-section seeds with skin microbes. Long-term effects studied (some contested).
contains_live_bacteria_prebiotics_hmos: Contains live bacteria + prebiotics (HMOs) that feed specific gut microbes (B. infantis).
sperm_egg_fuse_diploid_zygote_forms: Sperm + egg fuse. Diploid zygote forms.
rapid_cell_divisions_without_growth_2_: Rapid cell divisions without growth. 2 → 4 → 8 → 16 cell stage.
hollow_ball_with_inner_cell_mass_futur: Hollow ball with inner cell mass (future embryo) + trophoblast (future placenta).
cell_movements_form_3_germ_layers_ecto: Cell movements form 3 germ layers: ectoderm, mesoderm, endoderm.
neural_tube_forms_from_ectoderm_future: Neural tube forms from ectoderm. Future brain + spinal cord.
all_major_organs_begin_forming_heart_s: All major organs begin forming. Heart starts beating ~week 4-5.
growth_maturation_major_structures_alr: Growth + maturation. Major structures already formed.
cancer_cells_produce_their_own_growth_: Cancer cells produce their own growth signals or activate receptors constitutively.
tumor_suppressors_p53_rb_inactivated_c: Tumor suppressors (p53, Rb) inactivated → cells divide despite stop signals.
apoptosis_pathways_disabled_damaged_ce: Apoptosis pathways disabled. Damaged cells survive when they should die.
reactivate_telomerase_telomeres_mainta: Reactivate telomerase → telomeres maintained → unlimited divisions.
tumors_recruit_blood_vessels_to_feed_g: Tumors recruit blood vessels to feed growing mass.
cells_lose_adhesion_migrate_colonize_d: Cells lose adhesion, migrate, colonize distant tissues.
warburg_effect_tumors_use_glycolysis_e: Warburg effect: tumors use glycolysis even in oxygen presence.
tumors_hide_from_or_suppress_immune_su: Tumors hide from or suppress immune surveillance (PD-L1 expression).
chronic_inflammation_creates_a_permiss: Chronic inflammation creates a permissive environment.
accumulating_mutations_chromosomal_rea: Accumulating mutations + chromosomal rearrangements drive evolution.
fast_non_specific_skin_mucus_complemen: Fast, non-specific. Skin, mucus, complement, neutrophils, NK cells, macrophages.
slower_days_highly_specific_has_memory: Slower (days), highly specific, has memory. B cells (antibodies) + T cells.
produce_antibodies_each_b_cell_makes_o: Produce antibodies. Each B cell makes one specificity. Activated B cells become plasma cells.
coordinate_immune_response_hiv_target_: Coordinate immune response. HIV target. Tipped from naive to specialized subsets (Th1, Th2, Th17, Treg).
kill_infected_or_cancerous_cells_via_p: Kill infected or cancerous cells via perforin + granzyme.
y_shaped_proteins_5_classes_igg_most_a: Y-shaped proteins. 5 classes: IgG (most abundant), IgA (mucosal), IgM (initial), IgE (allergies + parasites), IgD (B-cell surface).
cell_surface_molecules_that_present_an: Cell-surface molecules that present antigens to T cells. MHC I (all cells, viral peptides). MHC II (immune cells, extracellular peptides).
plasma_proteins_that_punch_holes_in_pa: Plasma proteins that punch holes in pathogens + recruit phagocytes.
signaling_molecules_of_immune_system_i: Signaling molecules of immune system. Interferons (anti-viral), interleukins (coordinate), TNF (inflammation).
long_lived_b_t_cells_from_prior_exposu: Long-lived B + T cells from prior exposure. Basis of vaccines + lifelong immunity.
train_adaptive_immunity_without_diseas: Train adaptive immunity without disease. mRNA (COVID), protein subunit, attenuated live, inactivated.
immune_system_attacks_self_type_1_diab: Immune system attacks self. Type 1 diabetes, RA, MS, lupus, Hashimoto's.
ige_mediated_hypersensitivity_mast_cel: IgE-mediated hypersensitivity. Mast cells release histamine.
dendrites_receive_soma_cell_body_axon_: Dendrites (receive), soma (cell body), axon (transmit), terminals (release neurotransmitter).
all_or_none_electrical_signal_sodium_i: All-or-none electrical signal. Sodium in → depolarize → potassium out → repolarize. ~1 ms. Traveling speed: ~1 m/s (unmyelinated) to ~100 m/s (myelinated).
fatty_insulation_made_by_glia_oligoden: Fatty insulation made by glia (oligodendrocytes in CNS, Schwann cells in PNS). Speeds conduction. Destroyed in MS.
junction_between_neurons_electrical_or: Junction between neurons. Electrical or chemical (most). ~0.02 μm gap.
chemical_messengers_across_synapse_exc: Chemical messengers across synapse. Excitatory (glutamate), inhibitory (GABA), modulatory (serotonin, dopamine).
reward_motivation_motor_control_low_in: Reward, motivation, motor control. Low in Parkinson's; dysregulated in schizophrenia.
mood_sleep_appetite_ssris_target_reupt: Mood, sleep, appetite. SSRIs target reuptake.
neuromuscular_junction_memory_attentio: Neuromuscular junction. Memory + attention. Low in Alzheimer's.
astrocytes_support_bbb_oligodendrocyte: Astrocytes (support, BBB), oligodendrocytes (myelin in CNS), microglia (immune), ependymal cells (CSF).
cerebrum_cortex_thinking_perception_ce: Cerebrum (cortex — thinking, perception), cerebellum (motor coordination), brainstem (autonomic), limbic system (emotion + memory).
brain_reorganizes_itself_strongest_in_: Brain reorganizes itself — strongest in childhood but ongoing throughout life. Basis of learning.
tight_junctions_between_brain_capillar: Tight junctions between brain capillary cells keep most molecules out. Major challenge for CNS drug delivery.
20_of_body_s_energy_at_rest_despite_be: ~20% of body's energy at rest despite being ~2% of mass. Mostly Na+/K+ pumps maintaining ion gradients.
first_mass_produced_human_protein_made: First mass-produced human protein. Made in E. coli (1982). Replaced pig + cow insulin.
single_target_therapies_humira_autoimm: Single-target therapies. Humira (autoimmune), Herceptin (HER2+ breast cancer), Keytruda (immunotherapy).
covid_19_vaccines_pfizer_moderna_self_: COVID-19 vaccines (Pfizer, Moderna). Self-amplifying versions + non-COVID applications in development.
replace_defective_genes_luxturna_inher: Replace defective genes. Luxturna (inherited blindness, FDA 2017). Zolgensma (SMA, 2019).
engineered_immune_cells_yescarta_kymri: Engineered immune cells. Yescarta + Kymriah for blood cancers. ~$400K per dose.
bone_marrow_transplants_standard_for_d: Bone marrow transplants standard for decades. Newer iPSC + ESC approaches in trials.
print_tissue_with_living_cells_scaffol: Print tissue with living cells + scaffolds. Skin, cartilage, bladder. Whole organs still distant.
engineer_biological_systems_bacteria_t: Engineer biological systems. Bacteria that produce drugs, yeast that brews opioids, algae biofuels.
bt_corn_insect_resistance_roundup_read: Bt corn (insect resistance), Roundup Ready (herbicide), Golden Rice (vitamin A).
str_profiling_mtdna_degraded_samples_y: STR profiling, mtDNA (degraded samples), Y-chromosome (paternal lineage). Database matching.
snp_arrays_23andme_ancestrydna_reveals: SNP arrays (23andMe, AncestryDNA). Reveals biogeographic ancestry + DNA relatives.
detect_cancer_dna_in_blood_earlier_dia: Detect cancer DNA in blood. Earlier diagnosis, treatment monitoring.
treat_solid_tumors_til_therapy_iovance: Treat solid tumors. TIL therapy (Iovance, melanoma) FDA approved 2024.
engineered_microbes_clean_up_oil_spill: Engineered microbes clean up oil spills, plastic pollution, heavy metals.
amplifies_specific_dna_sequences_a_bil: Amplifies specific DNA sequences a billion-fold. Workhorse of molecular biology, diagnostics, and forensics.
originally_a_bacterial_immune_system_n: Originally a bacterial immune system; now a programmable gene-editing tool. Doudna + Charpentier shared 2020 Nobel Prize.
viruses_are_non_living_infectious_agen: Viruses are non-living infectious agents — they need a host cell to replicate. Classified by genome type (Baltimore classification), envelope, shape.
microbiome_science_is_advancing_fast_m: Microbiome science is advancing fast. Many popular claims (specific probiotic strains "curing" specific conditions) outrun the evidence. Stick to robustly replicated findings.
defined_by_hanahan_weinberg_2000_updat: Defined by Hanahan + Weinberg (2000, updated 2011). Most cancers acquire most of these hallmarks. Therapy increasingly targets specific hallmarks.
